title: eriogonum ochrocephalum var. gracilipes
intro: Eriogonum ochrocephalum var. gracilipes, also known as the Utah State Flower, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States, particularly in the regions of Utah and Arizona. It belongs to the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae) and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, due to its high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Herbal preparations of Eriogonum ochrocephalum var. gracilipes can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal benefits.
